Scenario.overview: Energy Complex The HomeLand Justice group targets the energy sector with a sophisticated cyber attack plan, utilizing AI-powered reconnaissance, exploitation of public-facing applications, and lateral movement to achieve their objectives.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Windows Registry Command & Control Server Application Log Target System Artifact User Account Process Network Traffic Driver
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email Delivery Method Windows Registry Key Access (Mechanism) Web Shell Application Log Content (Mechanism) Anti-Debugging or Anti-Analysis User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Reg.exe Utility File Access (Mechanism) Network Traffic Content (Mechanism) Driver Load (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers utilize artificial intelligence to gather publicly available information about the energy company's infrastructure, employees, and systems.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers set up command and control (C2) servers and tools for future attacks by acquiring infrastructure through internet scans.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers target specific employees with phishing emails to obtain their login credentials.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-226: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers use obtained credentials to gain access to the energy company's network.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC-650: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: The attackers establish a persistent backdoor on the compromised system.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers take advantage of vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to escalate privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers evade detection by preventing analysis of malicious code.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers attempt to guess passwords using brute force techniques.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers identify and map the energy company's network.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attackers move laterally within the network using valid accounts.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from compromised systems.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate stolen data through FTP.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers destroy or corrupt critical systems and data.

Scenario.summary: The HomeLand Justice group executes a complex cyber attack against the energy sector, employing advanced tactics like AI-powered reconnaissance, exploitation of public-facing applications, and lateral movement to achieve their objectives.
